rulings.Case Title: WILL FREDERICKS ET AL VS. ROBERT FELTER ET AL
rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for October 22, 2025. Line 1.3. CROSS DEFENDANT SOTHEBY'S INTERNATIONAL REALTY, INC., SCOTT BRITTAIN DEMURRER to Amended CROSS COMPLAINT is SUSTAINED with leave to amend to allege facts supporting each element of the two causes of action asserted in the amended cross-complaint, including but not limited to, duty, affirmative misrepresentation, and reasonable reliance. Cross-Complaint shall not include any new causes of action without leave of court. =(501/CFH) Parties may appear in-person, telephonically or via Zoom (Video - Webinar ID: 160 560 5023; Password: 172849; or Phone Dial in: (669) 254-5252; Webinar ID: 160 560 5023; Password: 172849). Parties who intend to appear at the hearing must give notice to opposing parties and the court promptly, but no later than 4:00 p.m. the court day before the hearing unless the tentative ruling has specified that a hearing is required. Notice of contesting a tentative ruling shall be provided by sending an email to the court to Department501ContestTR@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. A party may not argue at the hearing if the opposing party is not so notified, and the opposing party does not appear.

rulings.Calendar Matter: MOTION FOR SUMMARY ADJUDICATION Of Issues Of Duty Against Cross-Defendants Compass California Ii, Inc., Pete Brannigan, And Silvia Zeng

CROSS COMPLAINANT ROBERT FELTER MOTION FOR SUMMARY ADJUDICATION Of Issues Of Duty Against Cross-Defendants Compass California Ii, Inc., Pete Brannigan, And Silvia Zeng is DENIED. rulings.Case Title: CONAN LI VS. OMED HAMID ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: MOTION FOR JUDGMENT ON THE PLEADINGS
rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for October 22, 2025. Line 7. PLAINTIFF CONAN LI MOTION FOR JUDGMENT ON THE PLEADINGS is DENIED. Plaintiff has not shown that the Complaint states facts sufficient to constitute a cause of action and the Answer does not state facts sufficient to constitute a defense to the Complaint. (CCP 438(c)(1)(A).) This is a two-part standard, and the first part is not addressed in Plaintiff's motion. Defendants' late-filed opposition is stricken, but was reviewed. =(501/CFH) Parties may appear in-person, telephonically or via Zoom (Video - Webinar ID: 160 560 5023; Password: 172849; or Phone Dial in: (669) 254-5252; Webinar ID: 160 560 5023; Password: 172849). Notice of contesting a tentative ruling shall be provided by sending an email to the court to Department501ContestTR@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. A party may not argue at the hearing if the opposing party is not so notified, and the opposing party does not appear.

rulings.Calendar Matter: Motion To Compel Further Responses To Interrogatories And Requests For Production Of Documents And For Monetary Sanctions

DEFENDANT WING LEUNG, LAI LEUNG, LAN CHIU Motion To Compel Further Responses To Interrogatories And Requests For Production Of Documents And For Monetary Sanctions HEARING REQUIRED.
